question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by making the mirror node return a nodesEnvelope with Links.Next pointing to a URL on a different domain, causing fetchMirrorNodeNetwork to follow cross-origin redirects and leak the request to an attacker-controlled server that returns a poisoned node list?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of IP address validation on serviceEndpoint.IPAddressV4 to inject RFC 1918 private addresses (10.x.x.x, 192.168.x.x) into the network map, causing the pinger to attempt connections to internal network hosts and potentially enabling SSRF-based lateral movement?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by injecting a nodesEnvelope with a very large number of pages via Links.Next, causing fetchMirrorNodeNetwork to accumulate nodes across hundreds of pages and exhaust memory before returning a usable network map?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the backoff formula cfg.mirrorNodeClientBaseBackoff * time.Duration(1<<(attempt-1)) with a large mirrorNodeClientMaxRetries value (e.g., 62) to cause 1<<61 to overflow int64, producing a negative backoff duration that causes time.After to fire immediately and create a tight retry storm?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by making the mirror node REST endpoint return a nodesEnvelope where serviceEndpoint.DomainName and serviceEndpoint.IPAddressV4 are both empty strings, causing net.Dial to fail with an empty address and producing a network map of unreachable nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the http.Client redirect-following behavior to cause the mirror node REST request to be redirected to an internal metadata service (e.g., 169.254.169.254 on cloud providers), leaking cloud credentials that could then be used to further manipulate the network topology?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by injecting a nodesEnvelope where nodeEntry.NodeAccountID uses a shard/realm format (e.g., 1.2.3) that differs from the expected network's shard/realm, causing hiero.ClientForNetwork to build a map with account IDs from a different shard and fail all transaction routing?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the select statement between ctx.Done() and time.After(backoff) to cause a race where a context cancellation during backoff sleep is not detected until the next attempt, causing one extra unnecessary network request after shutdown that could expose credentials?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by making the mirror node return a nodesEnvelope with serviceEndpoint.Port set to the mirror node's own REST port, causing the pinger to send gRPC traffic to the REST API endpoint and receive HTTP responses that the gRPC client cannot parse?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the strings.HasSuffix(base, '/api/v1') check to inject a URL like 'http://attacker.com/api/v1' as cfg.mirrorRest, causing the network topology request to be sent to an attacker-controlled server that returns a poisoned nodesEnvelope without the /api/v1 suffix being appended again?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by making the mirror node REST endpoint return a nodesEnvelope with all nodes having identical ServiceEndpoints (same IP and port), causing the SDK to treat all consensus nodes as a single node and lose fault tolerance, making a single node failure a complete network partition?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of response Content-Type validation on the http.Client to cause the mirror node to return a non-JSON response (e.g., HTML error page) that causes json.Unmarshal to fail on every retry, exhausting all mirrorNodeClientMaxRetries attempts and returning a nil network map?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by injecting a nodesEnvelope where serviceEndpoint.IPAddressV4 contains IPv6 addresses formatted as IPv4 (e.g., ::ffff:192.0.2.1), causing net.Dial to fail or connect to unintended hosts depending on the SDK's address parsing behavior?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the http.Client's default redirect policy to cause the mirror node REST endpoint to issue a 301 redirect to an HTTP URL when the original request was HTTPS, downgrading the connection and enabling injection of a malicious nodesEnvelope via MITM?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by making the mirror node return a nodesEnvelope with a Links.Next value that is a relative URL, causing fetchMirrorNodeNetwork to construct an invalid absolute URL and fail all subsequent pagination requests, returning an incomplete node list that excludes critical consensus nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum node count limit in nodesEnvelope parsing to inject a response with 100,000 nodeEntry records, causing the network map passed to hiero.ClientForNetwork to be so large that node selection algorithms become O(n) and effectively stall all transaction submission?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by making the mirror node REST endpoint intermittently return valid responses followed by poisoned ones, exploiting the fact that buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes only calls fetchMirrorNodeNetwork once at startup and never refreshes the network map, locking the pinger into a stale or malicious topology?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fmt.Errorf('attempt %d/%d: %w', attempt, attempts, err) error wrapping to cause error messages containing sensitive URL or credential information from cfg.mirrorRest to be logged, leaking internal network topology details that aid further partition attacks?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by injecting a nodesEnvelope where serviceEndpoint.DomainName is set to a domain that resolves to a CNAME chain pointing to an attacker-controlled IP, bypassing IP-based allowlists and causing the pinger to connect to fake consensus nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the time.After(backoff) channel in the retry loop to cause a goroutine leak if the context is cancelled while waiting for backoff, since the timer is not explicitly stopped, accumulating leaked timers across multiple startup attempts and degrading performance?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by making the mirror node return a nodesEnvelope where all nodeEntry.ServiceEndpoints have DomainName set to 'localhost' and IPAddressV4 empty, causing the SDK to connect to the pinger's own localhost and route all transactions to a non-existent local gRPC server?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of HTTP response status code validation in fetchMirrorNodeNetwork to cause the mirror node to return HTTP 206 Partial Content with a truncated nodesEnvelope JSON body, causing json.Unmarshal to succeed on the partial data and build an incomplete network map missing critical nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by injecting a nodesEnvelope where nodeEntry.NodeAccountID is set to '0.0.0' (the zero account), causing hiero.ClientForNetwork to map all nodes to the zero account ID and fail all transaction routing due to invalid account references?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the http.Client without a custom DialContext to cause the mirror node REST request to be resolved via a compromised DNS resolver, redirecting the topology fetch to an attacker-controlled server and enabling full network map poisoning?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by making the mirror node return a nodesEnvelope with serviceEndpoint.Port set to negative values (e.g., -1), which may be accepted by JSON unmarshalling into the int Port field and cause net.Dial to fail with an invalid port error for all nodes?
